steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content:
The sample has $202+34=236$ voters planning to vote for Candidate A out of $400$ voters total.
$$\frac{236}{400}=\frac{59}{100}$$

steps.title: Scale to the town
steps.content: Apply that proportion to the town's $6{,}000$ voters. $$\frac{59}{100}\cdot 6{,}000=59\cdot60$$ $$59\cdot60=3{,}540$$ This is 3540 voters. The answer is 3,540.
